title: Immersive Focus Mode — Full-Page Card and Left-Side Actions
problem: Focus mode is not immersive enough: sibling cards stay visually present at full weight, the focused card doesn't claim the freed space, and action buttons on the right edge are easy to miss.
analysis: Two independent problems compound: the freed whitespace from hidden cards is wasted instead of being given to the focused card, and the thread/focus buttons belong near the content's left entry point, not the right edge. The fix expands the focused card to fill the column width and moves buttons to a left gutter. Dimming unfocused cards reinforces the spatial metaphor without removing them from the DOM.

decisions.decision: Focused card expands to fill 100% column width; unfocused cards render at reduced opacity (~30–40%) and remain non-interactive
decisions.rationale: Full column width makes the spatial shift legible without a modal overlay — the tree structure stays visible in the background, preserving provenance context.
alternativesConsidered: Modal/overlay for focus — hides the tree, breaking the provenance metaphor
CSS zoom on focused card — distorts layout and causes reflow

decisions.decision: Thread and focus buttons move to the left side of the card in a narrow 40px gutter
decisions.rationale: Left-side placement aligns with reading direction — the eye enters a card from the left, so navigation affordances placed there are encountered naturally before the content.
alternativesConsidered: Keep buttons on the right — users report not finding them
Buttons inline with title — clutters the title row
FAB pattern — ambiguous which card it targets

decisions.decision: Unfocused cards use CSS opacity + pointer-events: none, not display: none
decisions.rationale: Keeping them in the DOM lets the reader see the tree shape without being distracted by it — pointer-events: none prevents accidental clicks while ancestors remain reachable via breadcrumb.
alternativesConsidered: visibility: hidden — same as display:none for perception
Filter: blur + opacity — adds GPU cost with no benefit

decisions.decision: Tree starts fully expanded on load — pre-populating collapsedKeys was reverted
decisions.rationale: Starting collapsed was confusing: visitors saw an empty-looking tree and had to discover the expand affordance before seeing any content. Starting expanded trades initial visual density for immediate legibility.
alternativesConsidered: Start all collapsed — was the original design (every parent key pre-populated into collapsedKeys), reverted after it confused readers on first load
Remember collapse state via sessionStorage — moot once start-collapsed was reverted

implementation: FeatureCard accepts a focusState prop ('focused' | 'context' | 'dimmed' | 'normal'). The feature-entry wrapper renders with an inline paddingLeft based on depth. When focusState === 'dimmed', the card receives the CSS class 'is-dimmed', which applies opacity: 0.45 via App.css. Focused and context cards render at full opacity with no additional CSS classes. The depth-based paddingLeft from feature-entry is not overridden for focused cards — column-width expansion was designed but not implemented in code. Action buttons (expand, focus, json, graph) live in .card-footer at the bottom of the card; left-gutter button placement was designed but not implemented. collapsedKeys starts as an empty Set (fully expanded) — the original design pre-populated all parent keys but this was reversed because starting collapsed confused visitors. The collapse-all button in FilterBar still lets users collapse all parents at once.
successCriteria: Activating focus mode causes all non-focal cards (not focusKey, not ancestors, not direct children) to grey out to 0.45 opacity via .is-dimmed. Focused and context cards remain at full opacity. The '⊙ focus' button in the card footer shows card-action--active styling on the focused card. Clicking focus again (or '← all') clears focus and restores all cards to full opacity.
knownLimitations: Column-width expansion for focused cards and left-gutter button placement were designed but not built — focused cards retain their depth-based indent and buttons stay in card-footer
Dimmed opacity (0.45) is tuned for the warm cream palette — dark mode would need a different value
No pointer-events: none on dimmed cards — they remain interactive despite being visually greyed out

lineage.spawnReason: feat-2026-014 defined focus mode semantics; this feature defines focus mode visuals and fixes button placement.

statusHistory.reason: Multiple drifts vs actual code: (1) decision 4 says 'start collapsed' but code starts fully expanded; (2) implementation claims left-gutter buttons, full-column-width focused card, card-with-gutter wrapper, expanded padding+shadow — none of these exist. Actual: only .is-dimmed{opacity:0.45} is applied, focus button stays in card-footer, no CSS for focused card width change.

knownLimitations still reference unbuilt features: 'Left gutter reserves 40px' (no gutter exists) and 'opacity values (0.22 dimmed / 0.55 context)' (actual is 0.45 for .is-dimmed, no separate context opacity)

annotations.author: lac advance

revisions.author: claude
revisions.reason: Three drifts corrected: (1) Decision 4 updated — 'start collapsed' design was explicitly reverted; code starts with new Set() (expanded). (2) Implementation rewritten to match actual code: only .is-dimmed CSS class exists, no left-gutter buttons, no full-column-width for focused cards — those were designed but not built. (3) successCriteria updated to describe actual dimming behavior rather than unbuilt full-column layout.
